evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establishing documentation, version control, and ensuring data privacy compliance
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — documentation, version control, compliance, CI/CD standards — with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Global consumer brand BI role with a compliance mention (data privacy) but no financial reporting or regulatory-audit language, so moderate rather than high.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Promoting BI adoption and data literacy throughout EMEA
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Region-wide BI adoption efforts, established CI/CD standards, and a senior technical-priority-setting role reflect an established, specialized data organization operating at scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: Strong hands-on experience with Snowflake and DBT
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Snowflake, dbt) and specific practices (CI/CD standards, ETL projects) make the day-to-day work reasonably clear, though several bullets are broad ('Supporting teams through troubleshooting and training').
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Promoting BI adoption and data literacy throughout EMEA
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on enabling BI adoption and self-service data literacy internally, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Translating business requirements into technical solutions and setting technical priorities
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role sets technical priorities and establishes standards region-wide, a clearly direction-setting/strategic framing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Experience establishing CI/CD standards for data engineering
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: CI/CD appears as a requirement/experience item without an explicit ownership verb framing a formal testing practice in responsibilities.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: explicit compliance language ('ensuring data privacy compliance') appears among the core responsibilities, meeting the compliance/regulatory risk signal.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Dominant verbs are optimizing, enhancing, and managing existing BI infrastructure rather than building new systems from scratch.
evidence.loss_aversion: ensuring data privacy compliance — explicit compliance framing among core responsibilities.
